sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
ACTION7 is not a valid action (error: "Unknown action at index 1: 'ACTION7'"). But it's listed in valid_actions. Strange.
Also, no G/g overlays were detected in the ascii output after selecting (19,36). But the segmentation shows:
- W: center=(0,56), pixels=14 (white node at top right)
- W: center=(30,31), pixels=660 (large white node - probably the background)
- b: center=(45,49), pixels=63 (blue node - probably the bottom grid border)
- b: center=(30,21), pixels=23 (blue node - probably the top grid border)
So there are no G/g overlays detected. This is strange because I can see them in the image.
Wait, looking at the image again, I see:
- A G overlay around (19,36) - the selected green cell
- A g overlay at (19,42) - to the RIGHT of the selected cell
But the ascii output doesn't show any G/g characters. Let me check the ascii output more carefully.
Actually, the ascii output only showed G/g for rows that have them. Since no rows were printed, there are no G/g characters in the ascii.
But the segmentation shows no G/g nodes either. So the overlays are not being detected.
Wait, maybe the overlays are rendered as part of the green cells, not as separate nodes. Let me check the green nodes more carefully.
Actually, looking at the segmentation output from earlier:
- After selecting (19,24), there were G/g nodes detected
- After selecting (19,36), there are no G/g nodes detected
This is strange. Let me check if the board state is different.
Actually, I think the issue is that the overlays are only visible when I select a green cell that has other green cells to its left. When I select (19,36), which is the rightmost green cell, there are no green cells to its right, so the overlay is different.
Let me try selecting (19,18) which is the leftmost green cell, and see what happens.
Actually, let me try a completely different approach. Let me look at the history to understand what actions led to the level being completed (if it was ever completed).
Looking at the history, the level was never completed. The best I achieved was reducing the green cells to 3 at column 42, but then clicking on the purple cursor reset the board.
Let me try the merge sequence again, but this time I'll be more careful:
1. Select (19,18) - the leftmost green cell
2. See where the g overlay appears
3. Click on the g overlay
Let me try this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Select (19,18) and check for overlays
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 18}])

# Check ascii for G/g overlays
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(64):
    row = ascii_lines[r]
    special = [(i, c) for i, c in enumerate(row) if c in 'Gg']
    if special:
        print(f"Row {r} G/g: {special}")

# Check segmentation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g', 'N'):
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
        print(f"{n['color']}: center=({cr},{cc}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
